description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with a single "build" stage that runs on the docker image and uses the docker:dind service. For every job whose name starts with "build-" (e.g., build-alpine, build-alpine-edge, build-alpine-pages, build-alpine-seccomp, build-centos-latest, build-clang, build-coverity, build-cross-armhf, build-debian-oldstable, build-debian-stable, build-debian-stable-i386, build-debian-stable-mdns, build-debian-testing, build-debian-unstable, build-fedora-latest, build-gentoo, build-opensuse-leap, build-python2, build-python3, build-ubuntu-latest, build-ubuntu-rolling), each job runs in the build stage, uses the docker image, and has the rule "if: $CI_PIPELINE_SOURCE == \"schedule\"" so they only execute on scheduled pipelines. Each job sets IMAGE_NAME by stripping the "build-" prefix from its job name, logs in to the GitLab registry with $CI_REGISTRY_USER and $CI_REGISTRY_PASSWORD, attempts to pull the latest image from $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE/$IMAGE_NAME:latest (ignoring failures), builds the Docker image with "docker build --pull --cache-from $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE/$IMAGE_NAME:latest --rm=false --tag $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE/$IMAGE_NAME:latest . --file .dockerfiles/$IMAGE_NAME", and pushes the new image. The variable GIT_DEPTH is set to 1 for all jobs, and the jobs build-alpine-edge, build-cross-armhf, and build-debian-unstable are marked allow_failure: true. No caches, artifacts, or deployment targets are defined.
